description: The expected number of days a plant type stays in harvest, recorded on the plant_type term (farmOS label 'Days of harvest').
probe_semantics: Deliver the integer days-of-harvest recorded on the subject plant_type TERM (the term's harvest_days integer field), or the empty value when none was recorded.

given: a plant_type term with harvest_days set to 30
a second plant_type term with no harvest_days
then: days_to_harvest of the second term == the empty value

punts: kind='assertion' guessed deterministically from gap_kind='bundle_field'; field declared in a config YAML on the plant_type vocabulary term, not a log bundle

SHAPE DEVIATION from the propose-terms sketch: the proposal read the subject as 'a land asset' set via 'record_log with days_to_harvest set to 60' (the log-record shape the proposer defaults to). The plant_type planning fields live ON a plant_type TERM (farm_plant_type), not on any log — so the subject is a plant_type term (subject_kind=entity), created via the new plant_type `given` write surface (MetaCoding plant-type). No record_log is involved.

SHAPE DEVIATION (name): the glossary term days_to_harvest (the proposer's semantic name, consistent with the days_to_* lexicon family) reads the farmOS field harvest_days. The write-side GivenStep field keeps the source name (harvest_days); the read-side glossary term takes the semantic name — the laboratory-vs-lab house form. NOTE: the source label is 'Days OF harvest' (a duration in harvest), not 'days until harvest' as the proposer's description read; probe_semantics corrected accordingly.

DERIVATION VALIDATED LIVE (oracle taxonomy_term/plant_type, MetaCoding plant-type): harvest_days is delivered as a JSON INTEGER (30), not a string; an unset field is delivered as null, read back as the empty value "" (the lot_number house form).

STANDING-POLICY DECISION (BIND-WITH-FEATURE): treated as BIND-WITH-FEATURE(plant_type) under Duke's standing policy 'identity terms bind DURING their feature's port' (term-dispositions-2026-07-22.md) without a fresh per-term elicitation. Reversal condition: Duke may re-disposition at the next wave boundary; an unbound or retired term costs only its spec.
